description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 9: Equal Groups (Multiplication)

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent understanding of repeated addition, skip counting, and grouping. Can fast-track to multiplication concepts with challenge content

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to multiplication as equal groups
Making equal groups and repeated addition connection
Skip counting as a multiplication strategy

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of multiplication concepts

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to multiplication as equal groups
Making equal groups with repeated addition

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in repeated addition, skip counting, or grouping. Needs targeted review before multiplication concepts

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Grouping objects into equal groups
Start main topic: Multiplication as equal groups
Making equal groups with repeated addition

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in repeated addition, skip counting, and grouping before attempting multiplication

videoSequence.reason: Gentle intro to multiplication as equal groups
Begin main content: Making equal groups

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with multiplication.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

number_sense.conceptName: Basic number sense and understanding of numbers

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: There are 4 plates with 3 cookies on each plate. How many cookies are there in total?
questions.explanation: 4 plates with 3 cookies each: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 12 cookies. That is 4 x 3 = 12!

questions.question: 5 groups of 2 is the same as:
questions.explanation: 5 groups of 2 means adding 2 five times: 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 10. That is 5 x 2 = 10!

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand equal groups and multiplication very well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with equal groups concept, review repeated addition

questions.question: What is 6 x 3?
questions.explanation: 6 x 3 = 18. You can skip count by 3s: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18!

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for multiplication word problems

ifWrong.message: Watch the times tables video again and practise skip counting to build your multiplication facts.

skip_ahead.action: Skip practice video, jump to challenge or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on repeated addition

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising multiplication with real objects at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You know your addition and skip counting really well. You are ready to learn about multiplication. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand grouping and counting well. Let's learn about multiplication with complete coverage of all the topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in repeated addition or skip counting. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to multiplication.

low_score.message: You need to review repeated addition, skip counting, and grouping first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn multiplication.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how multiplication concepts build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

multiplicationFactsFluency.description: % of students who can solve basic multiplication facts within 10 seconds

equalGroupsUnderstanding.description: % of students who can model multiplication as equal groups

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 45-50 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 55-65 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (20 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 50-60 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasise that multiplication is about 'groups of' the same number, not just adding two different numbers. Use visual grouping examples.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students struggling to remember times tables
commonChallenges.solution2: Use skip counting as a strategy. For 4 x 3, skip count by 3s four times: 3, 6, 9, 12.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students who can skip count but cannot connect it to multiplication
commonChallenges.solution3: Use arrays to show the connection. 3 rows of 4 is skip counting by 4 three times: 4, 8, 12. So 3 x 4 = 12.
